transformation_type: deoxygenation of aldehydes and ketones to hydrocarbons
mechanism_type: hydrazone formation followed by base-promoted nitrogen extrusion and reduction to methylene
reactant_pattern_text: aldehyde or ketone + hydrazine or semicarbazide under strongly basic heating
common_conditions: hydrazone preformation then strong base and heat, often in high-boiling solvent or modified one-pot basic conditions
description_short: Hydrazone-based reduction that removes aldehyde or ketone oxygen under strongly basic conditions to give the corresponding hydrocarbon.

transformation_type: metal-mediated coupling of organohalides to carbon-carbon bonds
mechanism_type: sodium or related metal activation of halides followed by organometallic substitution and/or radical coupling
reactant_pattern_text: alkyl, allylic, benzylic, or aryl halide + sodium or finely divided metal
product_pattern_text: symmetrical or unsymmetrical alkane, cyclized hydrocarbon, or alkylated arene
common_conditions: metal-mediated coupling of halides, often under anhydrous conditions with heating, sonication, or solubilizing additive
description_short: Classical sodium-mediated coupling of organohalides used for dimerization, cross-coupling, and intramolecular ring closure of suitable dihalides.

transformation_type: high-dilution macrolactonization of hydroxy acids to medium or large ring lactones
mechanism_type: mixed anhydride formation with 2,4,6-trichlorobenzoyl chloride followed by DMAP-promoted intramolecular acyl transfer
reactant_pattern_text: hydroxy acid + 2,4,6-trichlorobenzoyl chloride + tertiary amine then DMAP under high dilution
common_conditions: mixed anhydride generation, filtration of ammonium salt, then slow addition to refluxing DMAP solution under high dilution
description_short: Macrocyclization method that converts hydroxy acids into medium- and large-ring lactones via mixed anhydrides and DMAP-catalyzed intramolecular acyl transfer.
